---
title: "05 - Frequentist vs Bayesian Model Comparison"
output: html_document
---

# Overview

This script compares the final frequentist and Bayesian logistic regression models.

The script:
- imports the final model results,
- compares odds ratios and uncertainty intervals,
- compares predicted probabilities,
- creates a selected-variable comparison table.

```{r}

# ==================================================
# Load packages and define paths
# ==================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(knitr)


freq_path <- file.path(
  "Results",
  "Frequentist Approach",
  "Pooling results"
)

bayes_path <- file.path(
  "Results",
  "Bayesian Approach"
)

fig_path <- "figures"
tables_path <- "Tables"

dir.create(fig_path, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(tables_path, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
```


```{r}
# ==================================================
# Load final model results
# ==================================================

freq_final_m5 <- read_csv(
  file.path(freq_path, "frequentist_selected_results_m5.csv")
)

freq_final_m10 <- read_csv(
  file.path(freq_path, "frequentist_selected_results_m10.csv")
)

bayes_final_m5 <- read_csv(
  file.path(bayes_path, "bayesian_final_results_m5.csv")
)

bayes_final_m10 <- read_csv(
  file.path(bayes_path, "bayesian_final_results_m10.csv")
)
```


```{r}
# ==================================================
# Prepare odds ratio comparison data
# ==================================================

clean_freq_results <- function(data, m_value) {
  data %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    transmute(
      Predictor = term,
      Method = "Frequentist",
      m = m_value,
      OR = estimate,
      Lower = conf.low,
      Upper = conf.high
    )
}

clean_bayes_results <- function(data, m_value) {
  data %>%
    filter(term != "b_Intercept", term != "Intercept") %>%
    mutate(term = gsub("^b_", "", term)) %>%
    transmute(
      Predictor = term,
      Method = "Bayesian",
      m = m_value,
      OR = OR,
      Lower = OR_lower,
      Upper = OR_upper
    )
}

freq_plot_m5 <- clean_freq_results(freq_final_m5, "m = 5")
freq_plot_m10 <- clean_freq_results(freq_final_m10, "m = 10")

bayes_plot_m5 <- clean_bayes_results(bayes_final_m5, "m = 5")
bayes_plot_m10 <- clean_bayes_results(bayes_final_m10, "m = 10")

# Clean predictor names

clean_predictor_labels <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    gsub("^SexM$", "Sex: Male", .) %>%
    gsub("^ChestPainTypeATA$", "Chest pain: ATA", .) %>%
    gsub("^ChestPainTypeNAP$", "Chest pain: NAP", .) %>%
    gsub("^ChestPainTypeTA$", "Chest pain: TA", .) %>%
    gsub("^FastingBS1$", "Fasting blood sugar", .) %>%
    gsub("^ExerciseAnginaY$", "Exercise angina", .) %>%
    gsub("^ST_SlopeFlat$", "ST slope: Flat", .) %>%
    gsub("^ST_SlopeUp$", "ST slope: Up", .)
}

or_comparison <- bind_rows(
  freq_plot_m5,
  bayes_plot_m5,
  freq_plot_m10,
  bayes_plot_m10
) %>%
  mutate(Predictor = clean_predictor_labels(Predictor))
```



```{r}
# ==================================================
# Compare odds ratios for m = 5 and m = 10
# ==================================================

or_plot_m5 <- or_comparison %>%
  filter(m == "m = 5") %>%
  mutate(Predictor = reorder(Predictor, OR)) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = OR,
      y = Predictor,
      xmin = Lower,
      xmax = Upper,
      shape = Method
    )
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_errorbarh(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.6),
    height = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.6),
    size = 2.5
  ) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of Odds Ratios for m = 5",
    x = "Odds Ratio (log scale)",
    y = "Predictor",
    shape = "Method"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

or_plot_m5

ggsave(
  file.path(fig_path, "comparison_odds_ratios_m5.png"),
  or_plot_m5,
  width = 9,
  height = 5.5,
  dpi = 300
)


or_plot_m10 <- or_comparison %>%
  filter(m == "m = 10") %>%
  mutate(Predictor = reorder(Predictor, OR)) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = OR,
      y = Predictor,
      xmin = Lower,
      xmax = Upper,
      shape = Method
    )
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_errorbarh(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.6),
    height = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.6),
    size = 2.5
  ) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of Odds Ratios for m = 10",
    x = "Odds Ratio (log scale)",
    y = "Predictor",
    shape = "Method"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

or_plot_m10

ggsave(
  file.path(fig_path, "comparison_odds_ratios_m10.png"),
  or_plot_m10,
  width = 9,
  height = 5.5,
  dpi = 300
)
```



```{r}
# ==================================================
# Load predicted probabilities
# ==================================================

freq_pred_m5 <- read_csv(
  file.path(freq_path, "frequentist_predicted_probabilities_m5.csv")
)

freq_pred_m10 <- read_csv(
  file.path(freq_path, "frequentist_predicted_probabilities_m10.csv")
)

bayes_pred_m5 <- read_csv(
  file.path(bayes_path, "bayesian_predicted_probabilities_avg_m5.csv")
)

bayes_pred_m10 <- read_csv(
  file.path(bayes_path, "bayesian_predicted_probabilities_avg_m10.csv")
)

```

```{r}
# ==================================================
# Prepare predicted probability comparison data
# ==================================================

freq_pred_m5_avg <- freq_pred_m5 %>%
  group_by(observation = row_number() %% 918, observed) %>%
  summarise(
    freq_prob = mean(predicted_probability),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    observation = ifelse(observation == 0, 918, observation)
  ) %>%
  arrange(observation)

freq_pred_m10_avg <- freq_pred_m10 %>%
  group_by(observation = row_number() %% 918, observed) %>%
  summarise(
    freq_prob = mean(predicted_probability),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    observation = ifelse(observation == 0, 918, observation)
  ) %>%
  arrange(observation)

# Combine predicted probabilities

pred_comparison_m5 <- freq_pred_m5_avg %>%
  select(
    observation,
    HeartDisease = observed,
    freq_prob
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    bayes_pred_m5 %>%
      select(
        observation,
        bayes_prob = predicted_probability
      ),
    by = "observation"
  ) %>%
  mutate(m = "m = 5")

pred_comparison_m10 <- freq_pred_m10_avg %>%
  select(
    observation,
    HeartDisease = observed,
    freq_prob
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    bayes_pred_m10 %>%
      select(
        observation,
        bayes_prob = predicted_probability
      ),
    by = "observation"
  ) %>%
  mutate(m = "m = 10")
```



```{r}
# ==================================================
# Compare predicted probabilities for m = 5 and m = 10
# ==================================================

pred_plot_m5 <- pred_comparison_m5 %>%
  mutate(
    HeartDisease = factor(
      HeartDisease,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("No Disease", "Disease")
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = freq_prob,
      y = bayes_prob,
      color = HeartDisease
    )
  ) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  geom_abline(
    slope = 1,
    intercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(),
    limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(),
    limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of Predicted Probabilities for m = 5",
    x = "Frequentist predicted probability",
    y = "Bayesian predicted probability",
    color = "Observed outcome"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

pred_plot_m5

ggsave(
  file.path(fig_path, "comparison_predicted_probabilities_m5.png"),
  pred_plot_m5,
  width = 10,
  height = 5.5,
  dpi = 300
)

# Predicted probability comparison for m = 10

pred_plot_m10 <- pred_comparison_m10 %>%
  mutate(
    HeartDisease = factor(
      HeartDisease,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("No Disease", "Disease")
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = freq_prob,
      y = bayes_prob,
      color = HeartDisease
    )
  ) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  geom_abline(
    slope = 1,
    intercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(),
    limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(),
    limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of Predicted Probabilities for m = 10",
    x = "Frequentist predicted probability",
    y = "Bayesian predicted probability",
    color = "Observed outcome"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

pred_plot_m10

ggsave(
  file.path(fig_path, "comparison_predicted_probabilities_m10.png"),
  pred_plot_m10,
  width = 10,
  height = 5.5,
  dpi = 300
)
```



```{r}
# ==================================================
# Create selected-variable comparison table
# ==================================================

selected_variables <- tibble(
  Predictor = c(
    "Age",
    "Sex",
    "ChestPainType",
    "RestingBP",
    "Cholesterol",
    "FastingBS",
    "RestingECG",
    "MaxHR",
    "ExerciseAngina",
    "Oldpeak",
    "ST_Slope"
  ),
  Frequentist = c(
    "Selected",
    "Selected",
    "Selected",
    "Not selected",
    "Not selected",
    "Selected",
    "Not selected",
    "Not selected",
    "Selected",
    "Selected",
    "Selected"
  ),
  Bayesian = c(
    "Not selected",
    "Selected",
    "Selected",
    "Not selected",
    "Not selected",
    "Selected",
    "Not selected",
    "Not selected",
    "Selected",
    "Selected",
    "Selected"
  )
)

selected_variables
```


```{r}
# ==================================================
# Save selected-variable comparison table
# ==================================================

write_csv(
  selected_variables,
  file.path(tables_path, "selected_variables_comparison.csv")
)

selected_variables_latex <- kable(
  selected_variables,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  caption = "Comparison of selected predictors in the frequentist and Bayesian final models."
)

writeLines(
  selected_variables_latex,
  file.path(tables_path, "selected_variables_comparison.tex")
)
```
